page: Amazon Web Services is4gen.2xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor using the ARM64 architecture. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance features 8 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.5 GHz with single-thread-per-core execution. The hardware configuration includes 48.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:6 vCPU-to-memory ratio, and a substantial 7500 GB local NVMe SSD storage array. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average performance across several core metrics, alongside some architectural limitations. The instance achieves average results in stress-ng multi-core CPU testing (12134.02) and Geekbench multi-core processing (5346.0). Memory bandwidth is notable, reaching 472.98 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, though large-block uncached writes drop to 55.65 GB/sec. Single-core performance is generally weaker, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 950.0 and weak ratings in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. Cryptographic capabilities are supported via hardware AES, though the processor lacks AVX or AMX vector extensions.

From a resource density perspective, the is4gen.2xlarge offers high local NVMe storage capacity relative to its compute footprint, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for storage-heavy workloads that do not require high single-thread CPU performance. Database operations and static web serving perform in the average tier, with Redis SET operations reaching 9.55 million operations per second. However, the instance is poorly suited for large language model inference, where text generation speeds fall into the weak to poor categories. Appropriate workloads include medium-scale databases, local storage caching, static web serving, and general-purpose storage-intensive applications.
description: Amazon Web Services is4gen.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with 48.0 GB of system memory and a 7500 GB local NVMe SSD. The instance delivers average multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12134.02, and average database performance, processing 9.55 million Redis SET operations per second. However, single-core capabilities are weaker, with a Geekbench single-core score of 950.0 and lower performance in compression tasks. Lacking advanced vector extensions like AVX or AMX, it is not designed for heavy mathematical acceleration or complex LLM inference. Instead, its high local storage density and balanced memory profile make it qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-intensive applications, database hosting, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services is4gen.2xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 server featuring 8 Graviton cores, 48 GB of RAM, and 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for database and storage-intensive workloads.

tagline: An ARM-based storage-optimized instance featuring high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage and balanced memory for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 cores running at 2.5 GHz
7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-capacity local data access
48.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:6 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Average performance in database operations and static web serving
Best suited for storage-intensive applications and database hosting
